ops.title: service: grow multi-agent support so synckit can compose N Agents
ops.description: synckit's `service/` package re-owns plist rendering and launchctl bootstrap/bootout (`ToolConfig{Agents []AgentSpec}`, `LaunchdManager`, its own `bootstrapWithRetry`) because `daemonkit/service.Agent` manages exactly one launchd job while synckit needs N LaunchAgents per tool (reconcile tick + serve daemon + consumer helpers). Grow `service` with a multi-agent composition surface, then migrate synckit/service onto it so the low-level launchd plumbing exists once. Found during the 2026-07-19 fleet-migration audit.

ops.text: daemonkit/service exposes a multi-agent composition surface (N Agents per tool config, installed/uninstalled coherently)
synckit/service drops its hand-rolled plist rendering and bootstrap retry in favor of daemonkit/service
